mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending corporate earnings declines, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving corporate revenue acceleration and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when either series reverts to trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks or demand destruction, prompting algorithmic selling and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered observations, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy during tightening regimes (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: The coexistence of near-zero policy rates and elevated unemployment indicates monetary policy transmission failure and structural economic weakness, leading to risk premium expansion and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined regime produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth provides a direct measure of aggregate economic activity that scales corporate earnings, generating a persistent positive drift in equity markets over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of observations following positive GDP prints, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.015 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to anticipate policy tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with p < 0.05 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly nonfarm payroll additions confirm labor market resilience and sustained consumer spending capacity, supporting near-term corporate revenue expectations and positive short-horizon equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations following positive payroll prints, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample tests.
